name: Hearty Pumpernickel Loaves
recipeIngredient: 1 square of Unsweetened Baking Chocolate, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine half the flour, half the rye flour, cornmeal, and undissolved yeast.
2. Heat water, molasses, oil, salt, and baking chocolate in a saucepan over low heat until very warm (120 to 130 Degrees F).
3. Add liquid mixture to the flour-yeast mixture. Beat with an electric mixer on high speed until smooth (about 3 minutes).
4. Add remaining rye flour and potatoes; beat for an additional 2 minutes.
5. Stir in caraway seeds and enough of the remaining flour to make a soft dough.
6. Turn dough onto a floured surface and knead until smooth and satiny (about 5 minutes). Dough will be soft and sticky.
7. Cover with a bowl and let rest for 20 minutes.
8. Divide dough into two halves; let rest for an additional 10 minutes.
9. Form each half into large balls and place on greased baking sheets.
10. Flatten loaves slightly with the palm of your hand and brush with oil.
11. Let rise in a warm place until doubled (about 30 minutes).
12. Preheat oven to 375 Degrees F.
13. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when tapped.
14. Remove from baking sheets and cool on wire racks until cold before slicing.
Makes 2 Loaves
